verses.latin_vulgate: Descende, sede in pulvere,  virgo filia Babylon :  sede in terra ; non est solium  filiæ Chaldæorum,  quia ultra non vocaberis  mollis et tenera.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רְדִ֣י ׀ וּשְׁבִ֣י עַל־עָפָ֗ר בְּתוּלַת֙ בַּת־בָּבֶ֔ל שְׁבִי־לָאָ֥רֶץ אֵין־כִּסֵּ֖א בַּת־כַּשְׂדִּ֑ים כִּ֣י לֹ֤א תוֹסִ֙יפִי֙ יִקְרְאוּ־לָ֔ךְ רַכָּ֖ה וַעֲנֻגָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Come down, and sit in the dust, O virgin daughter of Babylon, sit on the ground: there is no throne, O daughter of the Chaldeans: for thou shalt no more be called tender and delicate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tolle molam, et mole farinam ;  denuda turpitudinem tuam ;  discooperi humerum, revela crura,  transi flumina.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קְחִ֥י רֵחַ֖יִם וְטַ֣חֲנִי קָ֑מַח גַּלִּ֨י צַמָּתֵ֧ךְ חֶשְׂפִּי־שֹׁ֛בֶל גַּלִּי־שׁ֖וֹק עִבְרִ֥י נְהָרֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Take the millstones, and grind meal: uncover thy locks, make bare the leg, uncover the thigh, pass over the rivers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Revelabitur ignominia tua,  et videbitur opprobrium tuum ;  ultionem capiam, et non resistet mihi homo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּגָּל֙ עֶרְוָתֵ֔ךְ גַּ֥ם תֵּרָאֶ֖ה חֶרְפָּתֵ֑ךְ נָקָ֣ם אֶקָּ֔ח וְלֹ֥א אֶפְגַּ֖ע אָדָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Thy nakedness shall be uncovered, yea, thy shame shall be seen: I will take vengeance, and I will not meet thee as a man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Redemptor noster, Dominus exercituum nomen illius,  Sanctus Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גֹּאֲלֵ֕נוּ יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת שְׁמ֑וֹ קְד֖וֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: As for our redeemer, the Lord of hosts is his name, the Holy One of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sede tacens, et intra in tenebras,  filia Chaldæorum,  quia non vocaberis ultra  domina regnorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁבִ֥י דוּמָ֛ם וּבֹ֥אִי בַחֹ֖שֶׁךְ בַּת־כַּשְׂדִּ֑ים כִּ֣י לֹ֤א תוֹסִ֙יפִי֙ יִקְרְאוּ־לָ֔ךְ גְּבֶ֖רֶת מַמְלָכֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Sit thou silent, and get thee into darkness, O daughter of the Chaldeans: for thou shalt no more be called, The lady of kingdoms.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iratus sum super populum meum :  contaminavi hæreditatem meam,  et dedi eos in manu tua :  non posuisti eis misericordias ;  super senem aggravasti jugum tuum valde.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קָצַ֣פְתִּי עַל־עַמִּ֗י חִלַּ֙לְתִּי֙ נַחֲלָתִ֔י וָאֶתְּנֵ֖ם בְּיָדֵ֑ךְ לֹא־שַׂ֤מְתְּ לָהֶם֙ רַחֲמִ֔ים עַל־זָקֵ֕ן הִכְבַּ֥דְתְּ עֻלֵּ֖ךְ מְאֹֽד׃
verses.king_james: I was wroth with my people, I have polluted mine inheritance, and given them into thine hand: thou didst shew them no mercy; upon the ancient hast thou very heavily laid thy yoke.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixisti : In sempiternum ero domina.  Non posuisti hæc super cor tuum,  neque recordata es novissimi tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמְרִ֔י לְעוֹלָ֖ם אֶהְיֶ֣ה גְבָ֑רֶת עַ֣ד לֹא־שַׂ֥מְתְּ אֵ֙לֶּה֙ עַל־לִבֵּ֔ךְ לֹ֥א זָכַ֖רְתְּ אַחֲרִיתָֽהּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And thou saidst, I shall be a lady for ever: so that thou didst not lay these things to thy heart, neither didst remember the latter end of it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc audi hæc delicata,  et habitans confidenter,  quæ dicis in corde tuo :  Ego sum, et non est præter me amplius ;  non sedebo vidua,  et ignorabo sterilitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֞ה שִׁמְעִי־זֹ֤את עֲדִינָה֙ הַיּוֹשֶׁ֣בֶת לָבֶ֔טַח הָאֹֽמְרָה֙ בִּלְבָבָ֔הּ אֲנִ֖י וְאַפְסִ֣י ע֑וֹד לֹ֤א אֵשֵׁב֙ אַלְמָנָ֔ה וְלֹ֥א אֵדַ֖ע שְׁכֽוֹל׃
verses.king_james: Therefore hear now this, thou that art given to pleasures, that dwellest carelessly, that sayest in thine heart, I am, and none else beside me; I shall not sit as a widow, neither shall I know the loss of children:

verses.latin_vulgate: Venient tibi duo hæc  subito in die una,  sterilitas et viduitas :  universa venerunt super te,  propter multitudinem maleficiorum tuorum,  et propter duritiam incantatorum tuorum vehementem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְתָבֹ֩אנָה֩ לָּ֨ךְ שְׁתֵּי־אֵ֥לֶּה רֶ֛גַע בְּי֥וֹם אֶחָ֖ד שְׁכ֣וֹל וְאַלְמֹ֑ן כְּתֻמָּם֙ בָּ֣אוּ עָלַ֔יִךְ בְּרֹ֣ב כְּשָׁפַ֔יִךְ בְּעׇצְמַ֥ת חֲבָרַ֖יִךְ מְאֹֽד׃
verses.king_james: But these two things shall come to thee in a moment in one day, the loss of children, and widowhood: they shall come upon thee in their perfection for the multitude of thy sorceries, and for the great abundance of thine enchantments.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fiduciam habuisti in malitia tua,  et dixisti : Non est qui videat me.  Sapientia tua et scientia tua,  hæc decepit te.  Et dixisti in corde tuo :  Ego sum, et præter me non est altera.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּבְטְחִ֣י בְרָעָתֵ֗ךְ אָמַרְתְּ֙ אֵ֣ין רֹאָ֔נִי חׇכְמָתֵ֥ךְ וְדַעְתֵּ֖ךְ הִ֣יא שׁוֹבְבָ֑תֶךְ וַתֹּאמְרִ֣י בְלִבֵּ֔ךְ אֲנִ֖י וְאַפְסִ֥י עֽוֹד׃
verses.king_james: For thou hast trusted in thy wickedness: thou hast said, None seeth me. Thy wisdom and thy knowledge, it hath perverted thee; and thou hast said in thine heart, I am, and none else beside me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veniet super te malum,  et nescies ortum ejus ;  et irruet super te calamitas  quam non poteris expiare ;  veniet super te repente miseria  quam nescies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבָ֧א עָלַ֣יִךְ רָעָ֗ה לֹ֤א תֵֽדְעִי֙ שַׁחְרָ֔הּ וְתִפֹּ֤ל עָלַ֙יִךְ֙ הֹוָ֔ה לֹ֥א תוּכְלִ֖י כַּפְּרָ֑הּ וְתָבֹ֨א עָלַ֧יִךְ פִּתְאֹ֛ם שֹׁאָ֖ה לֹ֥א תֵדָֽעִי׃
verses.king_james: Therefore shall evil come upon thee; thou shalt not know from whence it riseth: and mischief shall fall upon thee; thou shalt not be able to put it off: and desolation shall come upon thee suddenly, which thou shalt not know.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sta cum incantatoribus tuis  et cum multitudine maleficiorum tuorum,  in quibus laborasti ab adolescentia tua,  si forte quod prosit tibi,  aut si possis fieri fortior.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִמְדִי־נָ֤א בַחֲבָרַ֙יִךְ֙ וּבְרֹ֣ב כְּשָׁפַ֔יִךְ בַּאֲשֶׁ֥ר יָגַ֖עַתְּ מִנְּעוּרָ֑יִךְ אוּלַ֛י תּוּכְלִ֥י הוֹעִ֖יל אוּלַ֥י תַּעֲרֽוֹצִי׃
verses.king_james: Stand now with thine enchantments, and with the multitude of thy sorceries, wherein thou hast laboured from thy youth; if so be thou shalt be able to profit, if so be thou mayest prevail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Defecisti in multitudine consiliorum tuorum.  Stent, et salvent te  augures cæli,  qui contemplabantur sidera,  et supputabant menses,  ut ex eis annuntiarent ventura tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִלְאֵ֖ית בְּרֹ֣ב עֲצָתָ֑יִךְ יַעַמְדוּ־נָ֨א וְיוֹשִׁיעֻ֜ךְ (הברו) [הֹבְרֵ֣י] שָׁמַ֗יִם הַחֹזִים֙ בַּכּ֣וֹכָבִ֔ים מֽוֹדִעִים֙ לֶחֳדָשִׁ֔ים מֵאֲשֶׁ֥ר יָבֹ֖אוּ עָלָֽיִךְ׃
verses.king_james: Thou art wearied in the multitude of thy counsels. Let now the astrologers, the stargazers, the monthly prognosticators, stand up, and save thee from these things that shall come upon thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce facti sunt quasi stipula,  ignis combussit eos ;  non liberabunt animam suam  de manu flammæ ;  non sunt prunæ quibus calefiant,  nec focus ut sedeant ad eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֨ה הָי֤וּ כְקַשׁ֙ אֵ֣שׁ שְׂרָפָ֔תַם לֹא־יַצִּ֥ילוּ אֶת־נַפְשָׁ֖ם מִיַּ֣ד לֶהָבָ֑ה אֵין־גַּחֶ֣לֶת לַחְמָ֔ם א֖וּר לָשֶׁ֥בֶת נֶגְדּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, they shall be as stubble; the fire shall burn them; they shall not deliver themselves from the power of the flame: there shall not be a coal to warm at, nor fire to sit before it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic facta sunt tibi in quibuscumque laboraveras :  negotiatores tui ab adolescentia tua,  unusquisque in via sua erraverunt ;  non est qui salvet te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּ֥ן הָיוּ־לָ֖ךְ אֲשֶׁ֣ר יָגָ֑עַתְּ סֹחֲרַ֣יִךְ מִנְּעוּרַ֗יִךְ אִ֤ישׁ לְעֶבְרוֹ֙ תָּע֔וּ אֵ֖ין מוֹשִׁיעֵֽךְ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Thus shall they be unto thee with whom thou hast laboured, even thy merchants, from thy youth: they shall wander every one to his quarter; none shall save thee.
